result.meeting_context: A Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee public hearing conducted remotely under a declared emergency on November 9. The agenda covered a consent calendar continuance and three public hearing items: a shelter hours modification at 748 Mercy Street, a two‑year extension for 1747 W Evelyn Avenue, and a one‑year extension and vesting tentative map for 2645–2655 Fayette Drive.

topics.topic: Remote Hearing Protocol and Roll Call

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Lindsay Hagan opened the November 9 hearing, presiding as Assistant Community Development Director and Zoning Administrator. She stated the meeting was conducted under California Government Code provisions authorizing remote meetings during a declared emergency, with participation by video and the enabling resolution available from the City Clerk.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This meeting will be conducted in accordance with the California Government Code Section 5, 4953 E as authorized by resolution.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Hagan outlined public comment procedures: raise hand in Zoom or press star‑6 to unmute by phone, with comments limited to three minutes. She opened oral communications for non‑agenda items and, seeing no speakers, closed that public comment period.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : All right, seeing no one, I will close the public comment period for the oral communications.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Roll call confirmed participation: Nicole Wright (City Attorney’s Office) and Ed Arango (Public Works) were present, and Hagan noted she was present and acting as Zoning Administrator.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : I'm acting as the zoning administrator.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Consent Item 4.1 (350 Ellis St) proposed modifying a prior planned community and development review permit for minor site and facade changes on a 19.6‑acre office campus. The item was not heard, previously continued from October 26, and will be renoticed for a future hearing when ready; no action occurred.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This item will not be heard at this meeting and was previously continued from the October 26th Zoning Administrator hearing.

points.speaker: Aki Snelling
points.text: Planner Aki Snelling described a request by Trinity United Methodist Church and HomeFirst Services to modify a provisional use permit for the annual cold‑weather shelter (women and families, up to 50 persons) at 748 Mercy Street, extending daily hours to begin at 3:00 PM and end at 9:00 AM to align with school schedules.
points.source: Aki Snelling : The church and home for services are requesting to extend the hours of operation to begin at 3:00 PM rather than the current 4:00 PM allowed, and to end at 9:00 AM rather than the existing 6:30 AM daily.

points.speaker: Aki Snelling
points.text: Snelling reported the shelter operates roughly from Thanksgiving week through March 31 (~3.5 months); Building and Fire departments had no concerns. An October 17 meeting with the Old Mountain View Neighborhood Association drew supportive comments and no concerns. Staff found the project consistent and categorically exempt under CEQA Section 15301 and recommended approval with conditions.
points.source: Aki Snelling : A community meeting was also held with the Old Mountain View Neighborhood Association on October 17th. No speakers voiced any concerns, and all who spoke were in support of the project.

points.speaker: Kristi Blewis
points.text: Kristi Blewis, representing Los Altos United Methodist Church (owner of Trinity UMC), affirmed partnership with HomeFirst Services to provide cold‑weather shelter and supported extending hours to better accommodate families with school‑aged children before and after school.
points.source: Kristi Blewis : We're really happy to partner with Home First to provide housing for families during the cold weather months and eager to extend the hours to make it a better option for families with school aged children...

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Hagan approved the permit modification to extend shelter hours with staff‑recommended findings and conditions, noting alignment with typical school hours. A 10‑calendar‑day appeal period applies; follow‑up questions should be directed to Aki Snelling.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : I do make a motion to approve this item with the conditions that have been included in the findings report.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: The request sought a two‑year extension of previously approved planned community and development review permits for a new four‑story commercial building (28,090 sq ft office, 6,500 sq ft retail, roof terrace) with three levels of underground parking accessed via a future adjacent public garage on City Parking Lot 4.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This is a request for a two year permit extension for previously approved planned community permit and development review. Permit to construct a new four story commercial building with 28,090 square feet of office and 6500 square feet of retail and a roof terrace as well as 3 levels of underground parking access through a future adjacent public parking garage on City Parking lot 4...

points.speaker: Eric Anderson
points.text: Advanced Planning Manager Eric Anderson stated the project is consistent with the General Plan and Downtown Precise Plan and categorically exempt under CEQA Section 15332. Staff recommended approval based on alignment with the Downtown Parking Strategy (adopted November 9, 2021) and timely applicant responses; no prior public comments were received.
points.source: Eric Anderson : Staff's recommending approval of this extension based on the work that the applicant has done to prepare and submit proposed modifications to the project consistent with the Downtown Parking Strategy which was adopted by the City Council on November 9th, 2021... No public comment has been received regarding this extension request and so staff is recommending approval.

points.speaker: Tim McEnery
points.text: Applicant representative Tim McEnery (Marwood) thanked City staff for working with them on the extension request and offered no additional comments.
points.source: Tim McEnery : Just thank you for the support by the staff for working with us on this extension request.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Hagan approved the two‑year extension with conditions as drafted by staff and posted online. A 10‑day appeal period applies; follow‑up questions should be directed to Eric Anderson.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so with that I do make a motion to approve this with the conditions as drafted by staff and posted online.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: The request sought a one‑year extension for previously approved permits to construct a six‑story, 44‑unit condominium project with a State Density Bonus replacing six dwellings and a 6,900 sq ft commercial building; a provisional use permit for a roof deck and a heritage tree permit (relocate one, remove eight); and a vesting tentative map creating 44 condominium lots and one common lot.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This is a request for a one year permit extension for previously approved planned community permit and development review permit to construct a six story 44 unit condominium project... A provisional use permit for a roof deck and a heritage tree removal permit to relocate one and remove 8 heritage trees... as well as a vesting tentative map to create 44 condominium lots and one common lot on the project.

points.speaker: Jeffrey Tsumura
points.text: Project Planner Jeffrey Tsumura reported the project was approved November 17, 2020; the applicant prepared comprehensive building permit submittals and expects to submit within weeks. The project remains substantially unchanged, complies with the San Antonio Precise Plan, General Plan, State Density Bonus Law and Subdivision Map Act, and is covered by an Initial Study/Mitigated Negative Declaration. Staff recommended conditional approval.
points.source: Jeffrey Tsumura : The project was approved back in November 17th, 2020 and since then the applicant has worked diligently to prepare a comprehensive set for building permit submittal... which they expect to submit in the coming weeks.

points.speaker: Ed Arango
points.text: Subdivision Committee actions: Ed Arango moved and Nicole Wright seconded approval of the one‑year extension of the vesting tentative map, and roll call votes were aye. Hagan also moved to approve extensions of the planned community, development review, and heritage tree removal permits; a 10‑day appeal period applies.
points.source: Ed Arango : I'd like to make a motion to approve the one year extension of their previously approved vesting tentative map, the 2645 and 2655 Fayette Drive, as subject to the conditions of approval...

points.speaker: Wing Tam
points.text: Public commenter Wing Tam, representing NorCal Carpenters Union Local 405, urged developers to adopt labor standards on the discussed projects, specifically local hiring, healthcare benefits, and prevailing wages. He offered union assistance and provided contact information (wtam@nccrc.org).
points.source: Wing Tam : I want to let the developer know so help help us to to have a provenil wages, healthcare and local hiring for our Labor Standards... So my my e-mail is wtam@nccrc.org.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Hagan noted that a 10‑calendar‑day appeal period applies to the approvals and concluded the Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee public hearing of November 9 at 4:22 PM, directing follow‑up inquiries to the respective project planners.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : Otherwise, if anyone has any follow up questions, please contact Jeffrey and that will conclude our Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee public hearing of November 9th at 4:22.
